summary: Large ftcpuattack decompilation PR that replaced several placeholders with partial C implementations for CPU attack selection and special-case attack exclusion logic. The main gains were in src/melee/ft/ftcpuattack.c: ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, ftCo_800B5AB0, and ftCo_800B77E8 were implemented, with smaller matching cleanups in ftCo_800B8A9C and ftCo_800BB220 plus header/type updates. Bot output reported major progress but not full matches: ftCo_800B77E8 reached 88.33%, ftCo_800B4AB0 91.73%, ftCo_800B52AC 91.12%, ftCo_800B5AB0 93.60%, ftCo_800BB220 93.90%, and ftCo_800B8A9C 87.29%. There was no substantive human review text in the slice, so intent beyond the diff and bot report is weakly evidenced.
what_changed: Added #include <math_ppc.h> in ftcpuattack.c, alongside math.h, for the new floating-point/math-heavy implementations.
Expanded local ftCo_AttackEntry from mostly padding into named offset fields: cmd, x04, x08, x0C, x10, x14, weight, x1C, and x20.

Added local placeholder structs ftCo_x50_attr and ftCo_x50_t to model the object passed through cpu->x50, including velocity/position-like fields, gravity/terminal-velocity-like attrs, state xC0, xCC attrs pointer, and bounds fields xC1C through xC28.

Implemented ftCo_800B4AB0 as a CPU attack-list selector against a Fighter target: filters by CPU level and cooldown/exclusion arrays, predicts relative X/Y positions using velocity/gravity/terminal velocity, checks attack bounds against target ranges, then selects a candidate by weighted HSD_Randf.
Implemented ftCo_800B52AC as a close variant of ftCo_800B4AB0 with an extra reach parameter folded into horizontal and vertical range checks.
Implemented ftCo_800B5AB0 as an attack-list selector against the cpu->x50 object rather than a Fighter target, using ftCo_x50_t and ftCo_x50_attr fields for predicted relative motion and bounds.

Implemented ftCo_800B77E8 as a large fighter-kind switch that populates cpu->xCC_array with attack command IDs to avoid/consider based on line-of-sight/floor checks, facing direction, ground-or-air state, fighter kind, and Kirby panic-charge state.
Updated ftCo_800B8A9C so ftCo_800B5AB0 is called with cpu->x50 as an explicit second argument plus the existing per-kind attack table from Fighter_804D64FC->x14.
Changed ftCo_800B5AB0's public prototype in ftcpuattack.h from two arguments to three arguments.
Changed Fighter_x1A88_t::x50 in types.h from int to u32.
Adjusted ftCo_800B8A9C literals from 5.0f/1000.0f to double literals 5.0/1000.0 in one ftCo_800A0FB0 call, presumably for matching.

Improved ftCo_800BB220 matching by initializing temp_r31 at declaration, rewriting an item-kind condition as a switch over It_Kind_Star and It_Kind_Unk4, and splitting repeated hit capsule delta expressions into dx/dy/dz temporaries.
smart_moves: Kept incomplete external data structures local to ftcpuattack.c as offset-named placeholders rather than prematurely exporting speculative names.

Modeled the common attack-selection algorithm consistently across ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, and ftCo_800B5AB0: sentinel-terminated attack lists, CPU-level gating via x20, exclusion checks through xCC_array/xEC, optional allow-list behavior via xA8_array/xC8, and weighted random selection.
Used explicit near-zero checks around gravity and weight-sum division paths, matching the generated control flow and avoiding division-by-nearly-zero behavior in reconstructed C.
Preserved matching-sensitive floating-point behavior with f64 casts and double literals where needed in projectile/trajectory-style calculations.
Inserted __assert("ftcpuattack.c", line, "0") fallthrough guards after weighted-selection loops, reflecting original impossible-path assertions and helping codegen line-number matching.
For ftCo_800B77E8, used separate repeated locals such as f1/L1/n1/p1 through f10/L10/n10/p10, which is verbose but likely helped reproduce the original stack layout for a large switch.
In ftCo_800BB220, introduced local dx/dy/dz temporaries before constructing projected hit positions, a small but reusable expression-shaping tactic for matching.
decomp_lessons: When a decompiled function consumes a data table, expanding a local struct from padding into offset fields can unlock readable control flow without needing final semantic names.
For CPU AI selection code, first identify sentinel fields, weight fields, level gates, cooldown divisors, and command arrays; these often explain most of the loop structure.
Prediction code involving gravity, terminal velocity, and sqrtf is especially sensitive to f32/f64 promotion. Preserve explicit casts and literal suffix choices from the matching attempt.
Large fighter-kind switch functions may require intentionally duplicated locals and duplicated helper-call blocks to reproduce stack allocation and register pressure.

If a discovered argument is hidden behind a global or struct field, update both the callsite and header prototype together; here ftCo_800B5AB0 became Fighter*, void*, void* and ftCo_800B8A9C now passes cpu->x50 explicitly.
Do not over-name unknown CPU fields too early. This PR kept many Fighter_x1A88_t members and temporary structs offset-named, which is appropriate when semantics are inferred only from code shape.
Small source-level rewrites outside the primary decompiled functions, such as converting if/else to switch or hoisting struct pointers to declaration, can materially improve match percentages.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based ftCo_800Bxxxx names.
Unknown struct fields used offset-style names such as x04, x08, xC1C, xC20, xC24, xC28.
Temporary local structs were named for the surrounding subsystem and key offset, e.g. ftCo_x50_t and ftCo_x50_attr.
Fighter CPU data was consistently accessed through struct Fighter_x1A88_t* cpu = &fp->x1A88.
Stack arrays used stack-offset-like names such as sp3C, sp40, and sp34, consistent with decomp matching style.
Locals combined semantic hints where obvious, such as fpPredY, relPredY, rangeF, rangeB, halfRange, fpTermNeg, tgtTermNeg, sizeHalf, and yBound, while leaving uncertain fields offset-named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Included math_ppc.h and used sqrtf in gravity prediction paths.
Used explicit f64 casts around arithmetic subexpressions before casting back to f32.
Used double literals 0.5, 1.0, 5.0, and 1000.0 in places where float suffixes would change codegen.
Preserved near-zero comparisons as value < 0.00001f && value > -0.00001f rather than abstracting into a helper.
Used sentinel loops over attack entries with while (list->cmd != 0).
Copied selected ftCo_AttackEntry values into fixed-size stack arrays of 32 candidates before weighted random selection.
Used __assert with explicit file name and original line-number constants 0xFA, 0x1C5, and 0x26A for unreachable weighted-selection fallthroughs.
Shaped ftCo_800BB220 codegen by changing an if/else item-kind test into a switch and by splitting vector deltas into dx/dy/dz locals.
Duplicated Vec3/u32/int local groups in ftCo_800B77E8 rather than reusing one set, likely to match stack/register allocation.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing seven unmatched-item improvements, with the largest byte gains in ftCo_800B77E8, ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, and ftCo_800B5AB0.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for ftCo_800B4AB0, ftCo_800B52AC, and ftCo_800B5AB0 to see whether the remaining nonmatching percentages were completed.
Search for ftCo_x50_t or Fighter_x1A88_t::x50 to determine the real type and semantic name of the cpu->x50 object.
Search for ftCo_AttackEntry to see whether its offset fields x04/x08/x0C/x10/x14/x1C/x20 were later renamed semantically.
Search for ftCo_800B77E8 to see whether the repeated line-of-sight/floor-check patterns were later refactored or better documented.
